joy.label: Joy
joy.description: Good, unexpected fortune that occurs in safe and familiar environments. Motivates one to savour the joyful event and seek similar events in the future
joy.appraisal: a little expectedness, pleasantness, goal conduciveness, external causation, moral, and an increase self-esteem

sadness.label: Sadness
sadness.description: sadness is a cognitive and emotional response to an irrevocable loss or failure
sadness.appraisal: high unpleasantness, goal obstruction, and an external, impersonal cause, accompanied by a low sense of coping ability

fear.label: Fear
fear.description: Widely agreed to be an emotion, fear occurs post-stimulus, when we experience a threat to our physical safety
fear.appraisal: unexpected, unpleasant, slight unfairness, external causation, inability to cope, morality

anger.label: Anger
anger.description: Anger arises when we are blocked from pursuing a goal and/or treated unfairly; causes approach behavior
anger.appraisal: unexpected, unpleasant, goal obstruction, perceived unfairness, external causation, slightly immoral

disgust.label: Disgust
disgust.description: Disgust means “bad taste”(dis = opposite of; gusto = taste/pleasure). The cause of disgust can go beyond the mouth, to waste and moral violations.
disgust.appraisal: unexpected, unpleasant, goal obstruction, perceived unfairness, external causation, slightly immoral

surprise.label: Surprise
surprise.description: Surprise is a brief, neutral emotion triggered by an unexpected event. Unlike fear, it is not inherently negative; the valence of surprise depends entirely on what follows it. It quickly gives way to whatever emotion is appropriate to the surprising event.
surprise.appraisal: High unexpectedness, neutral pleasantness, uncertain goal relevance, external causation, low coping certainty
